name: Dummy
description:
# Conversation Summary

1. **Project Context:** User requested a shield badge for the `thymeleaf-basic-preview-vscode` project located at `thymeleaf-basic-preview-vscode`.

2. **Badge Specifications:** The badge needed to display "make with ❤️ by codx-junior" text using the custom color `#9b117d` (purple/magenta), following the shields.io badge format.

3. **Solution Provided:** Generated a complete markdown badge with proper URL encoding for special characters (heart emoji and spaces) that renders correctly on GitHub and other platforms.

4. **Implementation Instructions:** Provided clear guidance on where and how to add the badge to the project's README.md file with a clickable link.

5. **Visual Verification:** Displayed the badge output to confirm the appearance and color match the user's requirements before integration into the project documentation.

messages.content: Create a shield "make with ❤️ by codx-junior" like: ![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-dev--tool-orange) use this color #9b117d

# Shield Badge Creation

Based on your requirements, here's the shield badge with your specifications:

```markdown
[![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/make%20with%20%E2%9D%A4%EF%B8%8F%20by-codx--junior-9b117d)](https://github.com/codx-junior)
```

## Visual Output
![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/make%20with%20%E2%9D%A4%EF%B8%8F%20by-codx--junior-9b117d)

## How to use this badge

Add this to your **README.md** file in the project:

```markdown /README.md
[![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/make%20with%20%E2%9D%A4%EF%B8%8F%20by-codx--junior-9b117d)](https://github.com/codx-junior)
```

## Badge Details
- **Left text:** `make with ❤️ by`
- **Right text:** `codx-junior`
- **Color:** `#9b117d` (elegant purple/magenta)
- **Style:** Flat (default shields.io style)

The URL encoding converts:
- `❤️` → `%E2%9D%A4%EF%B8%8F`
- `space` → `%20`
- `-` → `--` (for display purposes)

You can customize the link destination by changing the outer `href` parameter if needed!
